results.title: Remote Work Guidance for Cross-Border Employees
results.snippet: Best practices for managing remote employees working across borders, including time-zone overlap, local regulation compliance, and data security.

pages.title: Remote Work Guidance for Cross-Border Employees
pages.content:
## Remote Work Guidance for Cross-Border Employees

When allowing employees to work remotely from outside the United States, organizations should adhere to the following best practices to ensure smooth operations and legal compliance.

### 1. Time Zone Overlap
Employees should maintain a minimum 4-hour overlap with US Eastern Time core business hours (9:00 AM – 5:00 PM ET). This overlap enables real-time collaboration, attendance at team meetings, and timely communication with stakeholders.

### 2. Local Law Compliance
Verify that remote work arrangements comply with local employment laws, tax regulations, and social security requirements in the employee's country of residence. This may include:
- Registering the employer in the foreign jurisdiction
- Ensuring proper withholding of local income taxes
- Complying with local labor standards (e.g., working hours, leave entitlements)
- Adhering to data protection laws such as GDPR (EU) or LGPD (Brazil)

### 3. Data Security and Privacy
Require employees to use a company-approved VPN and encrypted devices. Sensitive company data should not be stored on personal devices or accessed over public Wi-Fi without security measures. Implement multi-factor authentication and regular security training.

### 4. Communication and Collaboration
Define clear communication protocols, including expected response times and availability hours. Use collaboration platforms (e.g., Slack, Microsoft Teams) and ensure all meetings are scheduled within the overlapping time window.

### 5. Equipment and Workspace
Provide necessary hardware (laptop, monitor, headset) and ensure the remote workspace meets ergonomic standards. Conduct periodic assessments of the home office setup for security and productivity.

### 6. Periodic On-Site Visits
Whenever possible and permitted by local immigration rules, schedule occasional visits to the main office for team building, training, and alignment with company culture. These visits may be subject to visa and business travel regulations.

For more detailed information, refer to the internal policy document "KB-REMOTE-POLICY" and consult with Human Resources and Legal departments.
